title: uputmess
text: uputmess Put a message in the Uniface message frame. Explain this code void uputmess (unsigned char*message); Copy Parameters message String containing the message to send Return Values uputmess does not return a value. Use Library: yrtl Description uputmess copies the text in message to the Uniface message frame. To create separate lines, you must use a carriage return (CR, ASCII code 13) as the line separator rather than a line feed (LF, ASCII code 10). If you pass a long text without line separators, Uniface will break lines after 1024 characters. Note that, because the line separator is a CR, not a LF, you cannot dump the contents of a text file to the message frame. You must read the individual lines, remove the line separators, and call uputmess on every line, as shown in the example. Example The following example reads lines from a file and writes them to the Uniface message frame one by one: Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> #include <stdio.h> #include <ctype.h> EXPORT void FileToMessFrame() { FILE* fp; char line[81]; char* p; fp = fopen("myfile.txt", "r"); while ( fgets(line, 81, fp) ) { for ( p=line; isprint(*p); p++ ); *p = 0; uputmess(line); } fclose(fp); } Copy Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
